scenarios.scenario-1: Unified release builds into a runnable container image with both portal and proxy capabilities.
scenarios.scenario-2: Dependency or assembly conflicts fail during build rather than at first request.
scenarios.scenario-3: User-facing Erlang sources containing Cyrillic literals compile into readable UTF-8 runtime responses.
scenarios.scenario-4: Release boot injects the per-SNI secret salt into `mtproto_proxy` before listener startup.

critical-invariants.invariant-1: One build artifact contains all required runtime components.
critical-invariants.invariant-2: Release config can be injected from env at runtime.
critical-invariants.invariant-3: Build and boot settings preserve readable UTF-8 user-facing text.

side-effects.effect-1: Builds container image and release artifacts.

redaction-rules.rule-1: Build logs must not bake secrets into image layers.

verification-levels.phase-level: Installer deploys a freshly built image successfully.

evidence.item: Per-SNI runtime probe compiles the release and asserts that the runtime getter reads `PROXY_SECRET_SALT` while `kpproton_app` injects `per_sni_secret_salt` and enables `per_sni_secrets` in `mtproto_proxy`.
UTF-8 runtime probe compiles the project and asserts readable Russian output from compiled email and verify modules.
`validate_rebar_project.sh` now also proves the governed `rebar.config` and `config/sys.config` files carry source-level MyGRACE contract metadata.

production-gates.gate-1: Release build and boot preserve Cyrillic text without mojibake.
production-gates.gate-2: The unified release injects `per_sni_secret_salt` and enables `per_sni_secrets` before public listener startup.
production-gates.gate-3: Release-level per-SNI secret enforcement remains aligned with derived-link issuance verification.
